title: cistus × ledon nothosubsp. atlantis
intro: Cistus × ledon nothosubsp. atlantis, also known as the Madeiran rockrose or Atlantic rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, native to the island of Madeira. It is valued for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which are due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and triterpenoids. The plant's medicinal properties make it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Cistus × ledon nothosubsp. atlantis, such as teas and infusions, are used to support the immune system and promote overall health.
